title: Sanofi
summary: Led Sanofi's migration from AWS to Snowflake — designing the new architecture and engineering an automated, bidirectional transfer layer so legacy systems on AWS kept running throughout. Zero operational downtime, reporting never interrupted.

beats.title: Infrastructure on AWS
beats.caption: Sanofi's data infrastructure lived on AWS, with legacy systems depending on it — nothing could go dark during a move.

beats.title: A bidirectional bridge
beats.caption: The key move: an automated, bidirectional transfer layer between AWS and Snowflake, keeping both sides in step in real time.

beats.title: New home: Snowflake
beats.caption: Data migrated into a new Snowflake architecture, while the bridge kept the AWS legacy systems working both ways.

beats.title: Reporting never stopped
beats.caption: Reporting ran uninterrupted through the entire transition — zero operational downtime.
